description: Speech is one way to 'sheori'; writing or a signed language may do the work instead. 'sheori' makes information or meaning available to another person, either one-way or reciprocally, without presuming understanding. 'mishoa' (express) can make an inner state perceptible without a recipient.
articulatory_notes: Raise the tongue blade towards the postalveolar region for voiceless 'sh' and pass air through the narrow channel into true-mid front 'e'. Keep stressed rounded true-mid back 'o' separate in hiatus. Tap or briefly trill the tongue at the alveolar ridge and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: A reciprocal exchange uses companion 'nua', while an intended recipient can take dative 'wei'. Information may appear as a direct object or a clause in 'mena ... meno'. In noun position, 'sheori' is communication.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Communication can cross a difference of medium while leaving receipt and understanding as questions in their own right.

semantic_domains.communication: meaning made available through a usable medium
semantic_domains.community: information exchanged among people through different forms of access
